observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant revise the presentation of Loans Payable in the financial statements, classifying the current and non-current portions in accordance with IPSAS 1 to ensure compliance with accounting standards and improve the accuracy and transparency of the financial statements.

We recommended to the management to enjoin the City Accounting Office to coordinate with the OCPS and the CEO to: a) identify and group accordingly which projects were fully implemented, used, and available for use; and b) make the necessary adjusting entries to correct recorded balances of PPE to conform to the aforementioned IPSAS.

We recommended that management ensure compliance with existing rules and regulations regarding the DRRMF, particularly concerning the transfer and utilization of unexpended balances, and:
Direct the relevant implementing offices to ensure timely submission of complete and necessary documentation to the CAO to facilitate the recognition of necessary journal entries; and
Enjoin the City Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reverse the incorrect transfer of DRRMF Capital Outlay.
We recommended the management to enjoin the City Accountant to prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected account balances.

We recommended to the management to enjoin the City Treasurer to:
Enforce the policy on daily remittance of collections from all collectors, accountable officers, and barangay treasures to designated Liquidating Officers through written communication to inform them of their responsibility on timely remittance of collections and include clear corrective actions to be taken in the event of non-compliance; and
Ensure all collections are deposited intact and in full on the next banking day, in strict adherence with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended to the management to:
Establish a process to ensure that the necessary preliminary activities are completed prior to the preparation of the Physical Inventory Plan and the conduct of inventory taking such as: (a) obtaining the latest RPCPPE, (b) update record of acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs, and (c) comparison and reconciliation of the latest RPCPPE/Inventory Report with the PPELCs/accounting records;
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a revised Physical Inventory Plan that includes specific, achievable timelines and mandate strict compliance with the plan; and
Establish an internal monitoring mechanism to track compliance with inventory taking requirements and prevent similar delays in the future.

We recommended to the management to:
Secure Certificate of Occupancy for the twenty-seven (27) infrastructure projects and submit copies to the COA audit team;
Instruct the City Engineering Office to include the cost of obtaining building permits as a pay item in the Program of Works; and
Require the contractors, in future vertical projects, to secure a building permit prior to construction and a Certificate of Occupancy prior to occupancy and use by the end-user.

We recommended to the management:
To safeguard the city’s assets, ensure coverage against potential damages, and avoid LTO penalties and fines, we recommended that management enjoin the CGSO to expedite the registration of all city-owned motor vehicles with the LTO and secure insurance for all city-owned motor vehicles; and
Failure to comply may result in the imposition of administrative sanctions on the accountable officers, if deemed warranted.

We recommended to the management to:
Enjoin the City Accountant to conduct review and verification of records to ascertain the validity of these long-outstanding accounts payable and facilitate their disposition or settlement. The verification process may include reviewing beneficiary records, contacting the beneficiaries, and coordinating with relevant offices; and
If thorough verification reveals no valid claimant for recorded accounts payable, consider returning the amount to the fund source and/or reverting to unappropriated surplus, whichever is applicable, in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended to the management to direct the City Accountant to cause the remittance of withheld premiums and loan amortization payments outstanding as of December 31, 2024 and strictly adhere to the provisions abovementioned to avoid penalties and surcharges that may be imposed against the City Government and its employees due to delayed remittances thereof.
We also recommended that the City Accountant verify the details of the carried-forward beginning balances of these inter-agency payables and prepare adjusting entries to correct the balances, where applicable.

We recommended that management allocate funds for the unpaid balance of the project Construction of Level III PWS considering that the undisbursed LGSF may not be utilized anymore and needs to be reverted to the National Treasury.
We also recommended the Management to require the Office of City Public Services to hasten the completion of the projects funded from LGSF NTF-ELCAC to fully attain the objectives of the program and enjoyment of the expected benefits, and to avoid risk of incurrence of additional expenses for the City for the uncompleted projects.

We recommended that management request the DBM and the DILG to extend the validity period of the undisbursed FY 2022 LGSF-FALGU funds to ensure the propriety and validity of charges against those funds.
If an extension is not granted, we recommended that management allocate funds to return the undisbursed balance to the National Treasury and ensure strict adherence to the aforementioned rules and regulations regarding the use of trust funds.

We recommended that management directs the responsible office, either the CPDO or the CEO to ensure the timely preparation and posting of the quarterly reports on fund utilization and the status of program/project implementation in accordance with DBM LBC Nos. 150 and 151.

We recommended that management optimize the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF allocation to enhance disaster risk reduction and management preparedness, in compliance with RA 10121.
We also recommended that management direct the CDRRMO to utilize the remaining cash donations in accordance with their general purpose. These programs, projects, and activities should be integrated into the LDRRMFIP pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions on updating the LDRRM Plan or LDRRMFIP to include PPAs charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
We also recommended that the management revert the unexpended balance that remained unutilized after the lapse of the prescribed five-year availability period of the Special Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and make such funds available for other social services subject to enactment by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

We recommended to the management to ensure strict compliance with the Procurement Law or RA 9184 and to:
Ensure all future procurement contracts for goods be covered with warranty security by either retention money of at least 1% but not to exceed 5%, or a special bank guarantee of the same percentage of the total contract price; and
Review and revise the current DV processing procedures, outlining the specific responsibilities of the CAO and the CGSO regarding the computation and withholding of retention money as warranty security for procured goods.

We recommended that Management consider evaluating the city's appropriations to identify potential funding sources for job order personnel wages, ensuring they meet the mandated minimum daily wage rates set by the Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Boards.

We recommended that management implement improvements to streamline job order payroll processing, to include among others: (a) develop a clear and concise checklist of required documents for job order wage processing, (b) establish and enforced fixed deadlines for the submission of all required documents, (c) designate specific personnel in each office/department to assist job order personnel with document preparation and submission, and (d) explore the feasibility of initial payroll preparation at the office/department level.

Instruct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to investigate and exert more effort in securing the documents and details of the unadjusted reconciling items; and
Ensure accurate and reliable information as to the details of the balances of the accounts in all Funds appearing in the GL and to reconcile same with the balances appearing in the BRS and Cashbooks maintained by the Cashier.

We recommended to management to provide provision of the estimated useful life of the aforementioned PPEs and direct City Accountant to make necessary journal entries to reflect the correct carrying amount of the PPE accounts.

We recommended to management to:
Enjoin the City Accountant, City Engineer, and CPS Officer to conduct reconciliation on the status of the projects recognized under the CIP accounts;
Direct the City Engineer and the CPS Officer to prepare and furnish the City Accountant the Certificates of Completion, Certificates of Acceptance and Inspection Reports for the completed projects, and prospectively enforce their submission as attachment to the Accomplishment Report; and
Direct the City Accountant to reclassify completed projects still recognized as CIP to its appropriate PPE account and provide depreciation based on the identified completion date.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the lapses in the recording on the books of accounts and adhere to the accounting procedures for prior period errors in the CY 2023 financial statements.

We recommended that management direct the City Accountant to complete the details of the accounts payable and confirm valid balances, otherwise revert to the General Fund those outstanding for two years or more and no actual claim filed either administratively or judicially, or not covered by perfected contracts on record in order to have fair presentation of accounts payable in the financial statements and re-appropriate additional funds for priority program, projects and activities of the City Government of Oroquieta.

We recommended that management direct the City Peace and Order Council to convene on this matter and exhaust efforts to liquidate the long outstanding cash advances for the Confidential Funds and make representation with the Office of the COA Chairperson and COA – ICFAU to seek assistance thereof in order to ensure that government funds are not exposed to the risks of loss or wastage.

We recommended that management:
Direct the City Planning and Development Coordinator to immediately comply with the documentary requirements as mandated by the DILG - Misamis Occidental Provincial Office to facilitate and expedite the implementation of the priority projects of LGSF-SBDP - NTF-ELCAC and LGSF-FALGU for the benefit of the concerned community and beneficiaries; and
Instruct the implementing offices to fast-track the implementation of the priority projects so that no appropriations will lapse on the validity period as prescribed under DBM LBCs for both Local Government Support Fund.

We recommended that management:
Instruct the City Engineer to ascertain the validity of the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities (PPAs) based on actual accomplishments of the projects;
Make representation with the DBM and DILG on the appropriate actions to be undertaken relative to the undisbursed fund balance; and
Direct the concerned member of the Local Finance Committee, in coordination with the City Accountant to strictly comply with the posting and reporting requirements in the utilization of the LGSF-FALGU pursuant to Section 6 of DBM LBC No. 142 dated January 24, 2022.

We recommended to management to:
Instruct the accountant to coordinate with the City Engineer’s Office to identify details of uncertain projects and make necessary journal entries to correct the Trust Liabilities -DRRM account;
Direct the Office of the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management to identify and allocate to priority projects all unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF and include such to the LDRRMFIP or revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund any unutilized amount those outstanding for more than five years; and
Enjoin the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, City Accountant and City Budget Officer to properly review correctness of Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the Notes to Financial Statements so that this will reconcile with the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations.

We recommended that management direct the City Assessor to come up with a strategy that would lay out courses of action to regularize the frequency of general revision of real property assessment and classification in accordance with Section 219 of Republic Act (R.A) No. 7160, and Section 1 of Chapter III of the Manual on Real Property Assessment effective CY 2023.

We recommended to management to:
Maximize the use of the biometric machine by registering all the JO personnel to the Biometric System and the printed copy of the biometrics records to be used to support their claims of wages to ensure the actual time work rendered without prejudice to a logbook maintained by each office, if any, as an added control procedure;
Consider installing additional biometric machines in strategic places or offices to accommodate the volume of personnel, that will be using the biometric machines with their place of assignment, if deemed necessary, considering the distance of their work area; and
Ensure attachment of the copy of the attendance logbooks or any form of documents that would indicate the attendance of personnel on fieldwork particularly those who are exempted from biometrics for validation purposes.

We recommended to management to:
Facilitate the adoption of automation of payroll or payroll-thru-bank system for payment of wages of the JO personnel to avoid security risks involving payrolls and to promote operational efficiency; and
Direct the HRMO to require the JO personnel without ATM cards to submit their application forms to the LBP as a requirement for subsequent issuance of ATM cards to them and coordinate with the LBP officials and employees for the processing of the same.

We recommended to management to:
Enjoin the Local School Board and Schools Division of Oroquieta City to submit justification why the purchased tumblers and reimbursed internet connectivity expenses were funded from the SEF through Supplemental Budget.; and
Instruct the Local School Board to strictly follow the prioritization of expenses in preparation of SEF Budget as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 so that funds will be spent for its intended purpose to benefit the entire school community.

We recommended to management to direct accountable officers to stop resorting to reimbursement in the incurrence of expenses and instead set up petty cash fund for petty expenses or draw special purpose cash advances for time-bound activities or resort to the alternative modes of procurement to comply with aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended to management to enjoin the Local School Board and the ECCD focal person to review the city’s programs, activities and projects and enforce allocation of funds from the Special Education Fund for the implementation of the ECCD programs in accordance with the provisions cited in RA No. 10410.

Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE);

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Require the City Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement on time pursuant COA Cir. 96-011 dated Oct. 2, 1996. Also, require the City Accountant to obtain copies of the debit/credit memos from the bank and effect the recording thereof including errors if any, to reflect the correct bank account balances.
Moreover, require the city accountant to take up in the books of accounts the quarterly interest earned on deposits and effect adjusting entries for the stale checks amounting to P 262,255.39 as of August 31, 2016.

We recommended to require the City Accountant to certify that funds previously transferred to the barangays/municipalities have been liquidated, post-audited and accounted for in the books, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We also recommended to require the Local Chief Executive to secure the approval or authorization by the sanggunian before incurring these expenses unless specifically described and the details thereof are sufficiently provided in the Annual and/or Supplemental Budgets.
Management Comment:
The management concurred with our findings and recommendations.

BAC should strictly observe the provisions of RA 9184 in the conduct of Public Bidding thus Contract/PO should not be awarded to ineligible/non-complying bidders.
Provisions of COA Cir. No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 should strictly be observed in the preparation and submission of Purchase Order. Purchase Order should invariably bear the complete specification including the brand name of the items to be procured which should be exactly the same as the specifications and brand name that should have been specified/stated in the Bid Proposal/Quotation of the bidder so declared as “winning bidder” to obtain the reasonable price or the lowest possible price.
Activity/Training Design showing in detail the specific scheduled dates of the seminar, the venue, the target participants (the barangays in each group/cluster & number of participants each) and the resource persons for each of the modules should be made prior to the conduct of training and the same should form part of the documents supporting the transaction.
The GSO, end users and BAC should exercise diligence to ensure that the Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC) reflects the most advantageous prevailing price to the Government as provided under Section 36 of the IRR.
The suggested retail price (SRP) thru preliminary canvass or from the Department of Industry or Department of Agriculture, Bureau of Agricultural Statistics depending on the nature of the items to be procured and the agency’s previous procurements should be secured in the preparation of the Purchase Request (PR) and ABC.
Likewise, unless observations Nos. 5.5, 5.6& 5.7 are justified, refund on the variance and or disallowable items is required.
We also recommended that management encourage and observe economy in the implementation of its programs/projects/activities. Commonly used supplies should be sourced from the Procurement Service whenever possible.

We recommended to require the BAC, through the Secretariat, to post, within three (3) calendar days from its issuance, the Notice of Award and within fifteen (15) calendar days from the issuance of the Notice to Proceed and the approved contract in the PhilGEPS and the website of the procuring entity, if any, pursuant to Sec. 37.1.6 and Section 37.4.2, respectively.

Require the management to strengthen/mobilize the GAD Focal Point System adhering to the structure and composition provided for in the MCW-IRR.
